Question: Suppose that $$\sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} x^{n}$$ has an interval of convergence of $$( -1 , 1 ) .$$ Find the interval of convergence of $$\sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} a_{n} ( \frac{x}{2} )^{n} .$$
Use the series for $$f ( x ) = \frac{1}{1 - x}$$ on $$| x | < 1$$ to construct a series for $$\frac{1}{( 1 - x ) ( x - 2 )} .$$ Determine the interval of convergence.
Answer: $$\sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} ( -1 + \frac{1}{2^{n + 1}} ) x^{n} .$$ The interval of convergence is $$( -1 , 1 ) .$$

Question: Find the function represented by the power series $$\sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} \frac{1}{3^{n}} x^{n} .$$ Determine its interval of convergence.
Answer: $$f ( x ) = \frac{3}{3 - x} .$$ The interval of convergence is $$( -3 , 3 ) .$$

Question: Multiply the series $$\frac{1}{1 - x} = \sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} x^{n}$$ by itself to construct a series for $$\frac{1}{( 1 - x ) ( 1 - x )} .$$

Differentiate the series $$\frac{1}{( 1 - x )^{2}} = \sum_{n = 0}^{\infty} ( n + 1 ) x^{n}$$ term-by-term to find a power series representation for $$\frac{2}{( 1 - x )^{3}}$$ on the interval $$( -1 , 1 ) .$$
Integrate the power series $$\text{ln} ( 1 + x ) = \sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} ( -1 )^{n + 1} \frac{x^{n}}{n}$$ term-by-term to evaluate $$\int \text{ln} ( 1 + x ) d x .$$
